Dear International School of the Middle East team,

Thank you for reaching out regarding your billing concern. Our finance team has been notified and will review your account within 4 hours.

If this is urgent, please reference ticket ID: TKT-1778526393690.

description: We have already paid the invoice a month ago but we still cannot access the School Support Hub and Direct. Please help

messages.text: We have already paid the invoice a month ago but we still cannot access the School Support Hub and Direct. Please help

Dear Egypt na Potpot,

Thank you for contacting us. We have received your technical support request and our team is investigating urgently.

Expected resolution: 2 hours.

We will keep you updated. Please do not submit duplicate tickets.

Best regards,
Support Team

subject: URGENT: Billing Discrepancy Escalation – Invoice #GUP-45821
description:
Dear GUP Finance Team,
Good day.
We are writing to seek clarification regarding Invoice #GUP-45821 dated 15 March 2026. Upon review, our Finance Department has identified a discrepancy in the total billed amount for student registrations under the A-Level programme.
Specifically:

We registered 148 students, but the invoice reflects billing for 162 students
We have attached our internal registration log and submission confirmation for your reference

We kindly request a reconciliation of this discrepancy and a revised invoice at your earliest convenience, as this impacts our internal budget approvals.
Regards,
Maria Santos
Finance Officer
St. Augustine International School

Email 2 – Initial Response (Provider → School)
Subject: RE: Clarification Required: Invoice #GUP-45821 Overcharge
Dear Ms. Santos,
Thank you for your email.
Based on our system records, the invoice was generated from the final submission data received on 10 March 2026. According to our logs, 162 student entries were processed.
We recommend verifying if multiple uploads or duplicate entries may have occurred during submission. If you can provide further details, we would be happy to review.
Kind regards,
James Holloway
Billing Support
Global University Partnerships

Email 3 – Escalation (School → Provider, Firmer Tone)
Subject: URGENT: Billing Discrepancy Escalation – Invoice #GUP-45821
Dear James,
Thank you for your response.
We have thoroughly reviewed our submission logs and can confirm:

Only one final submission was made
No duplicate student entries exist in our system
The attached confirmation report (timestamped 10 March 2026) shows exactly 148 students

Given this, we strongly believe the discrepancy originates from your system.
This matter is time-sensitive, as payment approvals are currently on hold. We request this case be escalated to your technical/billing investigation team immediately.
We expect a corrected invoice within 3 working days.
Regards,
Maria Santos

Email 4 – Internal Escalation Acknowledgement (Provider → School)
Subject: RE: URGENT: Billing Discrepancy Escalation – Invoice #GUP-45821
Dear Ms. Santos,
Thank you for your patience.
We have escalated your case to our Billing Investigation Team for further review. Initial findings suggest there may have been:

A system sync error resulting in duplicated entries during processing

We are currently conducting a full audit and will revert with a resolution shortly.
Kind regards,
James Holloway

Email 5 – Delayed Follow-up (School → Provider, Pressure Increases)
Subject: Follow-Up: Outstanding Billing Issue – Invoice #GUP-45821
Dear James,
We note that 5 working days have passed since your last update.
This delay is causing significant disruption to our financial processes. Unless this issue is resolved within the next 48 hours, we will need to escalate this matter to our Regional Partnership Manager.
Please provide a status update urgently.
Regards,
Maria Santos

Email 6 – Senior Escalation Response (Provider Manager → School)
Subject: Resolution Update: Invoice #GUP-45821
Dear Ms. Santos,
My name is Clara Bennett, Finance Operations Manager at GUP. I have taken ownership of this case.
Our investigation has confirmed:

A data duplication bug in our ingestion system
14 records were incorrectly counted twice

We sincerely apologize for the inconvenience caused.
Actions taken:

Revised Invoice #GUP-45821-A (attached)
Credit adjustment applied
Bug fix deployed to prevent recurrence

Please confirm once the revised invoice is acceptable.
Kind regards,
Clara Bennett
Finance Operations Manager

Email 7 – Closure (School → Provider)
Subject: RE: Resolution Update: Invoice #GUP-45821
Dear Clara,
Thank you for your prompt resolution and transparency.
We confirm that the revised invoice is accurate, and we will proceed with payment accordingly.
We appreciate your support in resolving this matter.
Regards,
Maria Santos
